ground_truth.title: What is Event Management? | JWU Online
ground_truth.author: JWU
ground_truth.main_content: Event management is defined as the process of planning and executing an entire event. It encompasses every stage of the event, from the initial conception of the idea until the event's completion. Event managers are in demand across various industries while organizations of all sizes attempt to plan events that are immersive, engaging, and memorable. These event professionals have an ability to coordinate the logistics, manage a budget, and think creatively in ways that set themselves apart from others in the hospitality field. According to openPR, the market for event management is expected to grow by $1.8 billion by 2029, as organizations and individuals alike seek to create compelling and cohesive events that make a long-lasting impression on attendees. Key components of event management include planning and budgeting, logistics and operations, and marketing and promotion. Essential skills needed include organization, communication, budgeting, negotiation, creativity, and technical skills. Technology is playing an increasingly prominent role in event management, with augmented reality and artificial intelligence being tools poised to define the industry. Career opportunities include corporate event planner, wedding planner, nonprofit event coordinator, trade show coordinator, convention planner, and sports and entertainment event planner. Pursuing a career in event management means investing in education and seeking professional experience, with a bachelor's degree program providing an advanced understanding of the event management industry along with in-demand industry skills.
with: Event management is defined as the process of planning and executing an entire event.
According to openPR, the market for event management is expected to grow by $1.8 billion by 2029.
Event managers are qualified professionals who have received extensive industry training.
Technology tools and digital platforms poised to define the industry include augmented reality and artificial intelligence.
An event management bachelor's degree program can provide an advanced understanding of the event management industry.
